source: Bank of Zambia (boz.zm), exchangerate.guru, exchange-rates.org, Wise, geocountries.com, tradingeconomics
baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held at 13.5% at May review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: BOZ official mid-rate (Jun 9): 17.7050. Market mid Jun 10: 17.80 (tradingeconomics, exchangerate.guru). Kwacha weakened 0.5% overnight — second day of retreat from Jun 4 low of 17.512. 7-day range: 17.51–18.20.

GBP.note: 1 GBP = 23.83 ZMW (exchangerate.guru, exchange-rates.org as of Jun 10). GBP/ZMW flat day-on-day. Cross via USD/GBP ~1.34.

EUR.note: 1 EUR = 20.5493 ZMW (geocountries.com) / 20.5386 (Wise) as of Jun 10. EUR/ZMW -0.1% day-on-day. 7-day high 20.8222 (Jun 3), low 20.236 (Jun 7). YTD decline -21.2%.

ZAR.note: 1 ZMW = 0.93 ZAR (exchangerate.guru Jun 10). 1 ZAR = 1.075 ZMW. Kwacha slightly weaker vs rand. 2026 high was 1 ZMW = 0.9422 ZAR on Jun 5 (valutafx).

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. Zambia's growing yuan-denominated mining tax corridor.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha retreats for second day — USD/ZMW up to 17.80 from Jun 4 low of 17.512
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: BOZ held MPR at 13.5% at May review. Inflation at 6.8% (April). Kwacha gave back some gains after hitting 2-year low of 17.512 on Jun 4 — profit-taking and possible BOZ smoothing. Further appreciation possible on copper tailwinds, but BOZ may intervene to protect export competitiveness. Key risks: global copper price reversal, drought cycles, election-related fiscal expansion (Aug 2026).
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Bias toward further appreciation if copper holds above $9,500/tonne and IMF successor programme progresses.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Wider range reflects election uncertainty (Aug 2026). Post-election policy continuity would support kwacha; fiscal slippage would reverse gains.
